str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - learning-resources
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: Overcoming the Fear of Starting Your First Real Project
entry.summary: In today's digital age, programming has become an essential skill for many individuals and businesses alike. However, starting your first real project can be overwhelming, especially if you're new to programming. In this blog post, we explore ten strategies that can help overcome the fear of starting your first real project and build the confidence you need to succeed. From defining your project goals and breaking them into smaller tasks to getting feedback, focusing on progress instead of perfection, practicing self-care, embracing failure as a learning opportunity, staying motivated, learning from experts, and being kind to yourself throughout the process, this post provides practical tips that will help you tackle your first project with confidence. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting out, these strategies can be applied to any programming language or framework. So, if you're feeling hesitant about taking the first step, read on and learn how to overcome your fears and achieve your goals in the world of programming!

entry.title: How to Deal with the Pressure of Learning New Technologies Quickly
entry.summary: In today's fast-paced tech landscape, it has become imperative for professionals across various industries to learn new technologies rapidly. Whether you are an entrepreneur, developer, or marketer, keeping up with technological advancements is vital to remain relevant and competitive in your field. In this blog post, we offer ten practical tips to help individuals navigate the pressure of learning new technologies quickly. The post commences by encouraging readers to define their learning goals clearly and create a roadmap to achieve them. It emphasizes the importance of staying informed about industry trends through attending conferences, webinars, forums, and online communities. Additionally, it highlights the need to set realistic expectations, choose learning resources wisely, practice consistently, collaborate with others, embrace failure as part of the learning process, take breaks when necessary, measure progress regularly, and stay positive and patient throughout the journey. By implementing these tips, individuals can accelerate their learning curve, gain mastery over new technologies, and remain competitive in their respective fields.
